title: Our Petty Cares
content:
Our petty cares wear on us so, -
    More cruel than our great despairs,
    More rasping than a mighty woe,
         Our petty cares.

    Less need of strength hath he who bears
    Courageously some stinging blow,
    Of Fate which takes him unawares.

    Not solitary griefs we know
    Induce old age and whitening hairs;
    But that malicious, endless row -
         Our petty cares.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Ella Wheeler Wilcox, titled "Our Petty Cares", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
